problems.name: Two Sum
problems.hint: For each number, check if its complement (target - num) was already seen — store seen numbers with their indices in a hash map.

problems.name: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
problems.hint: Track the minimum price seen so far. At each day, compute profit if you sold today — track the max profit.
problems.pseudocode:
minPrice = INF
maxProfit = 0
for price in prices:
    minPrice = min(minPrice, price)
    maxProfit = max(maxProfit, price - minPrice)
return maxProfit

problems.name: Contains Duplicate
problems.hint: Add each number to a set. If you ever see a number already in the set, return true.
problems.pseudocode:
seen = set()
for n in nums:
    if n in seen: return True
    seen.add(n)
return False

problems.name: Maximum Subarray (Kadane's)
problems.hint: At each index, decide: extend the previous subarray, or start fresh? Take the max. Track global max.
problems.pseudocode:
cur = best = nums[0]
for n in nums[1:]:
    cur = max(n, cur + n)
    best = max(best, cur)
return best

problems.name: Product of Array Except Self
problems.hint: Build prefix products and suffix products. answer[i] = prefix[i] * suffix[i]. Avoids division.
problems.pseudocode:
n = len(nums)
ans = [1] * n
left = 1
for i in range(n):
    ans[i] = left
    left *= nums[i]
right = 1
for i in range(n-1, -1, -1):
    ans[i] *= right
    right *= nums[i]
return ans

problems.name: 3Sum
problems.hint: Sort first. Fix one element, then use two pointers on the rest to find pairs summing to its negation. Skip duplicates.
problems.pseudocode:
nums.sort()
res = []
for i in range(n-2):
    if i>0 and nums[i]==nums[i-1]: continue
    l, r = i+1, n-1
    while l < r:
        s = nums[i]+nums[l]+nums[r]
        if s<0: l+=1
        elif s>0: r-=1
        else:
            res.append([nums[i],nums[l],nums[r]])
            while l<r and nums[l]==nums[l+1]: l+=1
            while l<r and nums[r]==nums[r-1]: r-=1
            l+=1; r-=1

problems.name: Container With Most Water
problems.hint: Two pointers at both ends. Compute area. Move the pointer at the SHORTER line inward (the only way area could increase).
problems.pseudocode:
l, r = 0, len-1
best = 0
while l < r:
    h = min(nums[l], nums[r])
    best = max(best, h * (r-l))
    if nums[l] < nums[r]: l += 1
    else: r -= 1
return best

problems.name: Maximum Product Subarray
problems.hint: Track both curMax and curMin (because a negative times a negative can become the new max).
problems.pseudocode:
curMax = curMin = best = nums[0]
for n in nums[1:]:
    candidates = (n, curMax*n, curMin*n)
    curMax = max(candidates)
    curMin = min(candidates)
    best = max(best, curMax)
return best

problems.name: Move Zeroes
problems.hint: Use a write pointer for the next non-zero position. After the loop, fill the rest with zeros.

problems.name: Sort Colors
problems.hint: Three pointers: low, mid, high. Swap based on nums[mid] value to partition 0s, 1s, 2s in one pass.

problems.name: Merge Intervals
problems.hint: Sort by start. For each interval, if it overlaps the last in result, merge; else append.

problems.name: Rotate Array
problems.hint: Three reverses: reverse whole array, reverse first k, reverse last n-k. O(1) space.
problems.pseudocode:
k = k % n
reverse(nums, 0, n-1)
reverse(nums, 0, k-1)
reverse(nums, k, n-1)

problems.name: Subarray Sum Equals K
problems.hint: Track running prefix sum in a hash map. At each step, check if (currentSum - k) was seen — that means a subarray ending here sums to k.

problems.name: Trapping Rain Water
problems.hint: Two pointers from both ends. Track leftMax and rightMax. Move the pointer whose side has smaller max — water trapped = max - current height.
problems.pseudocode:
l, r = 0, n-1
leftMax = rightMax = 0
water = 0
while l < r:
    if height[l] < height[r]:
        leftMax = max(leftMax, height[l])
        water += leftMax - height[l]
        l += 1
    else:
        rightMax = max(rightMax, height[r])
        water += rightMax - height[r]
        r -= 1
return water

problems.name: Find All Duplicates in Array
problems.hint: Use the array itself as a hash map: mark index (n-1) as negative when you see n. If already negative, n is a duplicate.

problems.name: Subarray Product Less Than K
problems.hint: Sliding window: expand right multiplying product; while product ≥ k, divide out nums[left] and shrink. Add (right-left+1) subarrays ending at right.
problems.pseudocode:
if k <= 1: return 0
prod = 1; ans = 0; l = 0
for r in range(len(nums)):
    prod *= nums[r]
    while prod >= k:
        prod //= nums[l]; l += 1
    ans += r - l + 1
return ans

problems.name: First Missing Positive
problems.hint: Cyclic sort: put each number x at index x-1 if 1 ≤ x ≤ n. Then scan for first mismatch.

problems.name: Summary Ranges
problems.hint: Walk through sorted array. Track start of current range. When gap > 1, close the range and start new.

problems.name: Valid Anagram
problems.hint: Count chars in s, decrement for t. If any count goes negative or any leftover, not an anagram.
problems.pseudocode:
if len(s)!=len(t): return False
count = {}
for c in s: count[c] = count.get(c,0)+1
for c in t:
    if c not in count or count[c]==0: return False
    count[c]-=1
return True

problems.name: Valid Palindrome
problems.hint: Two pointers from both ends. Skip non-alphanumeric. Compare lowercased chars.
problems.pseudocode:
l, r = 0, len(s)-1
while l < r:
    while l<r and not s[l].isalnum(): l+=1
    while l<r and not s[r].isalnum(): r-=1
    if s[l].lower()!=s[r].lower(): return False
    l+=1; r-=1
return True

problems.name: Reverse String
problems.hint: Two pointers at both ends, swap and move inward.
problems.pseudocode:
l, r = 0, len(s)-1
while l < r:
    s[l], s[r] = s[r], s[l]
    l+=1; r-=1

problems.name: First Unique Character
problems.hint: Count char frequencies, then find first index with count 1.

problems.name: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.hint: Sliding window with a set. Expand right; when a duplicate appears, shrink left until removed. Track max length.
problems.pseudocode:
seen = set(); l = 0; best = 0
for r in range(len(s)):
    while s[r] in seen:
        seen.remove(s[l]); l+=1
    seen.add(s[r])
    best = max(best, r-l+1)
return best

problems.name: Group Anagrams
problems.hint: Use sorted string as the hash key. All anagrams share the same sorted form.

problems.name: Longest Palindromic Substring
problems.hint: Try each center (2n-1 of them, including between chars). Expand outward while chars match.
problems.pseudocode:
def expand(l, r):
    while l>=0 and r<n and s[l]==s[r]: l-=1; r+=1
    return s[l+1:r]
best = ''
for i in range(len(s)):
    o = expand(i,i); e = expand(i,i+1)
    best = max(best, o, e, key=len)
return best

problems.name: Palindromic Substrings
problems.hint: Same as longest palindromic, but count every palindrome found during expansion.

problems.name: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
problems.hint: Sliding window. Window is valid if (window size - count of most frequent char) ≤ k. Track max frequency.
problems.pseudocode:
count = {}; l = 0; best = 0; maxFreq = 0
for r in range(len(s)):
    count[s[r]] = count.get(s[r],0)+1
    maxFreq = max(maxFreq, count[s[r]])
    while (r-l+1) - maxFreq > k:
        count[s[l]]-=1; l+=1
    best = max(best, r-l+1)
return best

problems.name: Find All Anagrams in String
problems.hint: Sliding window of size len(p). Compare frequency arrays. Slide by removing left char and adding right char.
problems.pseudocode:
if len(s)<len(p): return []
pCount = Counter(p); sCount = Counter(s[:len(p)])
res = [0] if sCount==pCount else []
for i in range(len(p), len(s)):
    sCount[s[i]] += 1
    sCount[s[i-len(p)]] -= 1
    if sCount[s[i-len(p)]]==0: del sCount[s[i-len(p)]]
    if sCount==pCount: res.append(i-len(p)+1)
return res

problems.name: String to Integer (atoi)
problems.hint: Skip whitespace, read sign, then read digits. Clamp to INT_MIN/MAX on overflow.
problems.pseudocode:
s = s.lstrip()
sign = 1; i = 0; num = 0
if s and s[0] in '+-':
    sign = -1 if s[0]=='-' else 1; i=1
while i<len(s) and s[i].isdigit():
    num = num*10 + int(s[i]); i+=1
    if num*sign > 2**31-1: return 2**31-1
    if num*sign < -2**31: return -2**31
return num*sign

problems.name: Minimum Window Substring
problems.hint: Expand right until window contains all of t. Then shrink left while still valid. Track min window.

problems.name: Roman to Integer
problems.hint: Map Roman chars to values. If a smaller value precedes a larger one (IV, IX), subtract it; otherwise add.

problems.name: Longest Common Prefix
problems.hint: Compare characters at each index across all strings. Stop at first mismatch.

problems.name: Two Sum
problems.hint: For each num, check if (target - num) was seen. Hash map stores num → index.

problems.name: Contains Duplicate
problems.hint: Add each num to a set. Return true if you ever see a duplicate.
problems.pseudocode:
seen = set()
for n in nums:
    if n in seen: return True
    seen.add(n)
return False

problems.name: Ransom Note
problems.hint: Count chars in magazine. For each char in ransom, decrement; fail if missing or zero.
problems.pseudocode:
count = Counter(magazine)
for c in ransomNote:
    if count[c]==0: return False
    count[c]-=1
return True

problems.name: Group Anagrams
problems.hint: Sorted form of an anagram is identical. Use it as the hash key.

problems.name: Top K Frequent Elements
problems.hint: Bucket sort: bucket[i] = list of elements appearing i times. Read from top.

problems.name: Longest Consecutive Sequence
problems.hint: Put all numbers in a set. For each, only start counting if (n-1) NOT in set (it's a sequence start).
problems.pseudocode:
nums_set = set(nums); best = 0
for n in nums_set:
    if n-1 not in nums_set:
        cur = n; streak = 1
        while cur+1 in nums_set: cur+=1; streak+=1
        best = max(best, streak)
return best

problems.name: Subarray Sum Equals K
problems.hint: Track running prefix sum. At each step, count how many earlier prefixes equal (currentSum - k).

problems.name: 4Sum II
problems.hint: Split into two pairs. Hash map of (A[i]+B[j]) → count. Then for each (C, D) pair, look up -(C+D).

problems.name: Valid Sudoku
problems.hint: Use three sets of seen: rows, cols, boxes. Box index = (r//3)*3 + c//3.
problems.pseudocode:
rows = [set() for _ in range(9)]
cols = [set() for _ in range(9)]
boxes = [set() for _ in range(9)]
for r in range(9):
    for c in range(9):
        v = board[r][c]
        if v=='.': continue
        b = (r//3)*3 + c//3
        if v in rows[r] or v in cols[c] or v in boxes[b]: return False
        rows[r].add(v); cols[c].add(v); boxes[b].add(v)
return True

problems.name: Reverse Linked List
problems.hint: Three pointers: prev, curr, next. Reverse each link as you walk.
problems.pseudocode:
prev = None; curr = head
while curr:
    nxt = curr.next
    curr.next = prev
    prev = curr
    curr = nxt
return prev

problems.name: Merge Two Sorted Lists
problems.hint: Dummy head. At each step, attach the smaller of the two list heads.

problems.name: Linked List Cycle
problems.hint: Tortoise and hare. If they meet, there's a cycle. If fast hits null, no cycle.
problems.pseudocode:
slow = fast = head
while fast and fast.next:
    slow = slow.next
    fast = fast.next.next
    if slow == fast: return True
return False

problems.name: Middle of Linked List
problems.hint: Fast moves 2 steps, slow moves 1. When fast hits end, slow is at middle.
problems.pseudocode:
slow = fast = head
while fast and fast.next:
    slow = slow.next
    fast = fast.next.next
return slow

problems.name: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List
problems.hint: Walk the list. If next node has same value, skip it.

problems.name: Remove Nth Node From End
problems.hint: Advance fast n steps first, then move both until fast hits end. Slow will be just before the node to remove.
problems.pseudocode:
dummy = Node(0); dummy.next = head
fast = slow = dummy
for _ in range(n): fast = fast.next
while fast.next:
    fast = fast.next; slow = slow.next
slow.next = slow.next.next
return dummy.next

problems.name: Reorder List
problems.hint: Find middle, reverse second half, then merge the two halves alternating.
problems.pseudocode:
# 1. Find middle (slow/fast)
# 2. Reverse from middle to end
# 3. Merge first half + reversed second half
# L0 → Ln → L1 → Ln-1 → ...

problems.name: Find the Duplicate Number
problems.hint: Treat nums[i] as pointer from i to nums[i]. Duplicate creates a cycle. Floyd's algorithm finds the entrance.
problems.pseudocode:
slow = fast = 0
do:
    slow = nums[slow]
    fast = nums[nums[fast]]
while slow != fast
slow = 0
while slow != fast:
    slow = nums[slow]; fast = nums[fast]
return slow

problems.name: Add Two Numbers
problems.hint: Walk both lists. Sum digits + carry. Push sum%10, carry = sum//10.
problems.pseudocode:
dummy = Node(0); cur = dummy; carry = 0
while l1 or l2 or carry:
    s = carry
    if l1: s += l1.val; l1 = l1.next
    if l2: s += l2.val; l2 = l2.next
    cur.next = Node(s % 10)
    carry = s // 10
    cur = cur.next
return dummy.next

problems.name: LRU Cache
problems.hint: Hash map for O(1) lookup, doubly linked list for O(1) reorder. Most recent at head, evict from tail.
problems.pseudocode:
# Map: key → node
# DLL: head (recent) ↔ ... ↔ tail (old)
# get(k): move node to head, return val
# put(k,v): if exists update+move to head
#         else add to head; if over cap, evict tail

problems.name: Merge K Sorted Lists
problems.hint: Min-heap of size k. Push all list heads. Pop smallest, append, push its next.

problems.name: Copy List with Random Pointer
problems.hint: Hash map: old node → new node. First pass create all nodes, second pass wire next/random.

problems.name: Rotate List
problems.hint: Make it circular, then break at the right spot. Length n, k %= n, new tail is at index n-k-1.
problems.pseudocode:
if not head or k==0: return head
# Compute length, link tail to head (circular)
# Walk (n - k%n - 1) steps to new tail
# New head = new_tail.next; new_tail.next = None

problems.name: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List II
problems.hint: Dummy head. Skip ALL nodes whose value appears more than once.

problems.name: Valid Parentheses
problems.hint: Push openers onto stack. On closer, pop and check match. Empty stack at end = valid.

problems.name: Implement Queue using Stacks
problems.hint: inStack for push, outStack for pop/peek. Pour in→out when out is empty.

problems.name: Next Greater Element I
problems.hint: Monotonic decreasing stack on nums2. For each popped element, record its next greater.

problems.name: Min Stack
problems.hint: Keep an auxiliary stack that tracks the minimum at each level. Or store (val, currentMin) tuples.
problems.pseudocode:
# Each stack element: (val, minSoFar)
push(x): stack.append((x, min(x, getMin())))
pop(): stack.pop()
top(): return stack[-1][0]
getMin(): return stack[-1][1]

problems.name: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
problems.hint: Push numbers. On operator, pop two, apply, push result.

problems.name: Generate Parentheses
problems.hint: Backtrack. Can add '(' if open < n. Can add ')' if close < open.

problems.name: Daily Temperatures
problems.hint: Monotonic decreasing stack of indices. When current temp > stack top, pop and record distance.
problems.pseudocode:
res = [0]*len(T); stack = []
for i, t in enumerate(T):
    while stack and T[stack[-1]] < t:
        j = stack.pop()
        res[j] = i - j
    stack.append(i)
return res

problems.name: Car Fleet
problems.hint: Sort by position desc. Compute time to target. If a car's time ≤ previous car's time, it joins the fleet.

problems.name: Decode String
problems.hint: Two stacks: counts and strings. On '[', push current state. On ']', pop and multiply.

problems.name: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
problems.hint: Monotonic increasing stack of indices. When a bar is shorter than stack top, pop and compute area with popped height.
problems.pseudocode:
stack = []; best = 0
for i in range(len(heights)+1):
    h = heights[i] if i<len(heights) else 0
    while stack and heights[stack[-1]] > h:
        H = heights[stack.pop()]
        W = i if not stack else i - stack[-1] - 1
        best = max(best, H*W)
    stack.append(i)
return best

problems.name: Basic Calculator
problems.hint: Stack handles parentheses and sign flips. Track current number and running sign.
problems.pseudocode:
# Process char by char:
# digit → build number
# '+'/'-' → push signed number to stack, reset
# '(' → push sign to stack
# ')' → sum stack until '(' marker
# Final: sum(stack)

problems.name: Asteroid Collision
problems.hint: Stack of surviving asteroids. New positive → push. New negative → collide with positives on top.

problems.name: Binary Search
problems.hint: Classic. lo=0, hi=n-1. Compare mid, discard half each iteration.
problems.pseudocode:
lo, hi = 0, len(nums)-1
while lo <= hi:
    mid = (lo+hi)//2
    if nums[mid]==target: return mid
    elif nums[mid]<target: lo = mid+1
    else: hi = mid-1
return -1

problems.name: First Bad Version
problems.hint: Binary search for leftmost bad version. If mid is bad, search left; else search right.
problems.pseudocode:
lo, hi = 1, n
while lo < hi:
    mid = (lo+hi)//2
    if isBadVersion(mid): hi = mid
    else: lo = mid+1
return lo

problems.name: Search Insert Position
problems.hint: Binary search. If not found, lo is the insert position.
problems.pseudocode:
lo, hi = 0, len(nums)
while lo < hi:
    mid = (lo+hi)//2
    if nums[mid] < target: lo = mid+1
    else: hi = mid
return lo

problems.name: Search a 2D Matrix
problems.hint: Treat 2D as 1D: index → (row=idx//cols, col=idx%cols). Standard binary search.
problems.pseudocode:
m, n = len(matrix), len(matrix[0])
lo, hi = 0, m*n - 1
while lo <= hi:
    mid = (lo+hi)//2
    v = matrix[mid//n][mid%n]
    if v==target: return True
    elif v<target: lo = mid+1
    else: hi = mid-1
return False

problems.name: Koko Eating Bananas
problems.hint: Binary search on eating speed k. Min k=1, max=max(piles). For each k, compute total hours; if ≤ h, try smaller.
problems.pseudocode:
lo, hi = 1, max(piles)
while lo < hi:
    mid = (lo+hi)//2
    hours = sum((p + mid-1)//mid for p in piles)
    if hours <= h: hi = mid
    else: lo = mid+1
return lo

problems.name: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
problems.hint: Compare mid with right. If nums[mid] > nums[right], min is in right half; else in left half.
problems.pseudocode:
lo, hi = 0, len(nums)-1
while lo < hi:
    mid = (lo+hi)//2
    if nums[mid] > nums[hi]: lo = mid+1
    else: hi = mid
return nums[lo]

problems.name: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
problems.hint: Identify which half is sorted, then decide if target is in that half.
problems.pseudocode:
lo, hi = 0, len(nums)-1
while lo <= hi:
    mid = (lo+hi)//2
    if nums[mid]==target: return mid
    if nums[lo]<=nums[mid]:  # left sorted
        if nums[lo]<=target<nums[mid]: hi=mid-1
        else: lo=mid+1
    else:  # right sorted
        if nums[mid]<target<=nums[hi]: lo=mid+1
        else: hi=mid-1
return -1

problems.name: Time Based Key-Value Store
problems.hint: Store values per key as list of (timestamp, value) sorted by timestamp. Binary search for largest timestamp ≤ input.
problems.pseudocode:
# set: store.append((timestamp, value))
# get: binary search for largest ts <= input
lo, hi = 0, len(store)-1
while lo <= hi:
    mid = (lo+hi)//2
    if store[mid][0] <= timestamp: lo = mid+1
    else: hi = mid-1
return store[hi][1] if hi>=0 else ''

problems.name: Find Peak Element
problems.hint: Compare mid with mid+1. If nums[mid] < nums[mid+1], peak is in right half; else left half.
problems.pseudocode:
lo, hi = 0, len(nums)-1
while lo < hi:
    mid = (lo+hi)//2
    if nums[mid] < nums[mid+1]: lo = mid+1
    else: hi = mid
return lo

problems.name: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
problems.hint: Binary search on smaller array. Find partition where left side has total (m+n+1)/2 elements.
problems.pseudocode:
# Binary search partition in smaller array
# Left has total (m+n+1)//2 elements
# Check: A[i-1] <= B[j] and B[j-1] <= A[i]
# If yes, found partition. Else adjust i.

problems.name: Split Array Largest Sum
problems.hint: Binary search on the answer (max subarray sum). Min=max(nums), Max=sum(nums). For each candidate, count needed splits.
problems.pseudocode:
lo, hi = max(nums), sum(nums)
while lo < hi:
    mid = (lo+hi)//2
    # Count subarrays needed if max sum = mid
    if feasible(mid): hi = mid
    else: lo = mid+1
return lo

problems.name: Capacity To Ship Packages Within D Days
problems.hint: Binary search on ship capacity. Min=max(weights), Max=sum(weights). For each capacity, count days needed.
problems.pseudocode:
lo, hi = max(weights), sum(weights)
while lo < hi:
    mid = (lo+hi)//2
    days = 1; cur = 0
    for w in weights:
        if cur+w > mid: days+=1; cur=0
        cur += w
    if days <= days_limit: hi = mid
    else: lo = mid+1
return lo

problems.name: Invert Binary Tree
problems.hint: Recursively swap left and right children. Base case: null node.

problems.name: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
problems.hint: 1 + max(depth(left), depth(right)). Base: null → 0.

problems.name: Same Tree
problems.hint: Both null → true. One null → false. Values differ → false. Else recurse left and right.

problems.name: Subtree of Another Tree
problems.hint: For each node in main tree, check if subtree from that node equals target tree.

problems.name: Symmetric Tree
problems.hint: Check if left subtree is mirror of right subtree. Two pointers (left, right) compared recursively.

problems.name: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
problems.hint: BFS with queue. Process entire level (queue size) at each iteration.

problems.name: Lowest Common Ancestor of BST
problems.hint: Walk down. First node where p and q diverge (one ≤ node ≤ other) is the LCA.
problems.pseudocode:
while root:
    if p.val < root.val and q.val < root.val: root = root.left
    elif p.val > root.val and q.val > root.val: root = root.right
    else: return root

problems.name: Binary Tree Right Side View
problems.hint: Level-order BFS, take the last node of each level.

problems.name: Count Good Nodes in Binary Tree
problems.hint: DFS with current max in path. A node is good if its value ≥ max so far.

problems.name: Validate Binary Search Tree
problems.hint: DFS with (min, max) bounds. Each node must be within its allowed range.

problems.name: Kth Smallest Element in BST
problems.hint: Inorder traversal of BST gives sorted order. Return the kth element visited.
problems.pseudocode:
def inorder(node):
    if not node: return
    inorder(node.left)
    count[0] += 1
    if count[0]==k: ans[0]=node.val; return
    inorder(node.right)

problems.name: Construct BT from Preorder & Inorder
problems.hint: preorder[0] is root. Find it in inorder to split into left/right subtrees. Recurse.
problems.pseudocode:
def build(pre, ino):
    if not pre: return None
    root = Node(pre[0])
    i = ino.index(pre[0])
    root.left = build(pre[1:i+1], ino[:i])
    root.right = build(pre[i+1:], ino[i+1:])
    return root

problems.name: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
problems.hint: At each node, compute max single-branch path (max(left, right, 0) + node.val). Update global answer with left+right+node.val.
problems.pseudocode:
best = -INF
def dfs(node):
    if not node: return 0
    l = max(0, dfs(node.left))
    r = max(0, dfs(node.right))
    best = max(best, l + r + node.val)  # path through node
    return max(l, r) + node.val  # single branch up
dfs(root); return best

problems.name: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
problems.hint: Use preorder traversal with null markers (e.g. 'N'). Rebuild recursively from the token stream.
problems.pseudocode:
# Serialize: preorder DFS, append 'N' for nulls
# Deserialize: read tokens one by one
#   if 'N': return None
#   else: build node, recurse left, recurse right

problems.name: Balanced Binary Tree
problems.hint: Return height if balanced, -1 if not. If any subtree returns -1, propagate up.
problems.pseudocode:
def check(node):
    if not node: return 0
    l = check(node.left)
    if l == -1: return -1
    r = check(node.right)
    if r == -1: return -1
    if abs(l-r) > 1: return -1
    return 1 + max(l, r)
return check(root) != -1

problems.name: Climbing Stairs
problems.hint: dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2] (Fibonacci). Space-optimize to two variables.
problems.pseudocode:
if n <= 2: return n
prev, cur = 1, 2
for _ in range(3, n+1):
    prev, cur = cur, prev+cur
return cur

problems.name: Fibonacci Number
problems.hint: fib(n) = fib(n-1) + fib(n-2). Bottom-up DP with two variables.
problems.pseudocode:
if n < 2: return n
a, b = 0, 1
for _ in range(2, n+1):
    a, b = b, a+b
return b

problems.name: Min Cost Climbing Stairs
problems.hint: dp[i] = cost[i] + min(dp[i-1], dp[i-2]). Answer = min of last two.

problems.name: House Robber
problems.hint: dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]). Rob current or skip.
problems.pseudocode:
prev, cur = 0, 0
for n in nums:
    prev, cur = cur, max(cur, prev + n)
return cur

problems.name: House Robber II
problems.hint: First and last can't both be robbed. Run house robber on nums[0:n-1] and nums[1:n], take max.

problems.name: Coin Change
problems.hint: dp[i] = min coins to make amount i. dp[i] = min(dp[i-coin] + 1) for each coin.

problems.name: Longest Increasing Subsequence
problems.hint: Patience sort: maintain tails array. Binary search to find position. Length of tails = LIS length.

problems.name: Unique Paths
problems.hint: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1]. First row/col = 1.

problems.name: Jump Game
problems.hint: Track maxReach. If current index > maxReach, return false.
problems.pseudocode:
maxReach = 0
for i in range(len(nums)):
    if i > maxReach: return False
    maxReach = max(maxReach, i + nums[i])
return True

problems.name: Decode Ways
problems.hint: dp[i] = number of ways to decode s[0..i]. Consider last 1 digit (if valid) + last 2 digits (if 10-26).
problems.pseudocode:
dp = [0]*(n+1); dp[0]=1; dp[1]=1 if s[0]!='0' else 0
for i in range(2, n+1):
    if s[i-1]!='0': dp[i] += dp[i-1]
    if 10<=int(s[i-2:i])<=26: dp[i] += dp[i-2]
return dp[n]

problems.name: Word Break
problems.hint: dp[i] = true if s[0..i-1] can be segmented. dp[i] = OR over j: dp[j] && s[j..i-1] in dict.

problems.name: Partition Equal Subset Sum
problems.hint: 0/1 knapsack with target = total/2. dp[w] = true if some subset sums to w.
problems.pseudocode:
if sum(nums)%2: return False
target = sum(nums)//2
dp = [False]*(target+1); dp[0]=True
for n in nums:
    for w in range(target, n-1, -1):
        dp[w] = dp[w] or dp[w-n]
return dp[target]

problems.name: Longest Common Subsequence
problems.hint: dp[i][j] = LCS of text1[0..i-1] and text2[0..j-1]. If chars match: 1+dp[i-1][j-1]. Else max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

problems.name: Edit Distance
problems.hint: dp[i][j] = edit distance between word1[0..i-1] and word2[0..j-1]. Min of insert/delete/replace.

problems.name: Burst Balloons
problems.hint: Interval DP. dp[i][j] = max coins from bursting balloons between i and j, with k as the LAST to burst.

problems.name: Triangle
problems.hint: Bottom-up DP. dp[i][j] = triangle[i][j] + min(dp[i+1][j], dp[i+1][j+1]). Optimize to 1D.

problems.name: Combination Sum IV
problems.hint: dp[i] = number of combinations summing to i. dp[i] = sum(dp[i-n] for n in nums if i>=n).

problems.name: Number of Islands
problems.hint: DFS from each unvisited '1' cell, marking all reachable '1's as visited. Count DFS invocations.

problems.name: Clone Graph
problems.hint: Hash map: old node → new node. BFS/DFS, create clones on demand.

problems.name: Max Area of Island
problems.hint: DFS returns area. Mark visited cells as 0. Track max area.

problems.name: Rotting Oranges
problems.hint: Multi-source BFS from all initially rotten oranges. Track minutes. Any fresh orange left at end = -1.

problems.name: Course Schedule
problems.hint: Topological sort via Kahn's algorithm. If we can process all courses, no cycle → true.

problems.hint: Same as Course Schedule I, but return the topological order. Empty array if cycle.
problems.pseudocode:

# Append each popped node to result
# If len(result) == n: return result
# Else: return []

problems.hint: Two DFS passes — one from Pacific border, one from Atlantic. Cells reachable from both = answer.
problems.pseudocode:

# DFS from atlantic-border cells (bottom row + right col)

problems.name: Surrounded Regions
problems.hint: DFS from border 'O's — mark them as safe. All other 'O's are surrounded → flip to 'X'.
problems.pseudocode:

# 2. Flip all remaining 'O' to 'X'
# 3. Flip 'S' back to 'O'

problems.name: Number of Connected Components
problems.hint: Union-Find. Initialize each node as own parent. Union edges. Count distinct roots.

problems.name: Redundant Connection
problems.hint: Union edges one by one. First edge whose endpoints are already in the same set = answer.
problems.pseudocode:
parent = list(range(n+1))
def find(x):
    while parent[x]!=x: parent[x]=parent[parent[x]]; x=parent[x]
    return x
for a,b in edges:
    ra, rb = find(a), find(b)
    if ra==rb: return [a,b]
    parent[ra] = rb

problems.name: Word Ladder
problems.hint: BFS from beginWord. Neighbors = words differing by one char. Track level.

problems.name: Alien Dictionary
problems.hint: Compare adjacent words to extract ordering rules. Build graph + in-degree. Topological sort.
problems.pseudocode:

# Else: concatenate order

problems.name: Evaluate Division
problems.hint: Build weighted graph: a→b weight=a/b, b→a weight=b/a. BFS/DFS from source to target, multiply weights.
problems.pseudocode:
graph = defaultdict(dict)
for a,b,v in equations:
    graph[a][b] = v
    graph[b][a] = 1/v
for q in queries:
    if src not in graph: res.append(-1); continue

problems.name: Network Delay Time
problems.hint: Dijkstra from source k. Answer = max distance to any node. If any unreachable, return -1.

problems.name: Subsets
problems.hint: At each index, choose to include or exclude. Recurse both ways, backtrack between branches.
problems.pseudocode:
res = []
def bt(start, path):
    res.append(path[:])
    for i in range(start, len(nums)):
        path.append(nums[i])
        bt(i+1, path)
        path.pop()
bt(0, [])
return res

problems.name: Permutations
problems.hint: Swap-based or used-array backtracking. Try every unused element at each position.
problems.pseudocode:
res = []
def bt(path, used):
    if len(path)==len(nums): res.append(path[:]); return
    for i in range(len(nums)):
        if used[i]: continue
        used[i]=True; path.append(nums[i])
        bt(path, used)
        path.pop(); used[i]=False
bt([], [False]*len(nums)); return res

problems.name: Combination Sum
problems.hint: Backtrack. Allow same element again (don't increment start). Stop when sum exceeds target.
problems.pseudocode:
res = []
def bt(start, path, total):
    if total==target: res.append(path[:]); return
    if total>target: return
    for i in range(start, len(candidates)):
        path.append(candidates[i])
        bt(i, path, total+candidates[i])  # i, not i+1 (reuse)
        path.pop()
bt(0, [], 0); return res

problems.name: Subsets II
problems.hint: Sort first. Skip duplicates: if i>start and nums[i]==nums[i-1], skip.
problems.pseudocode:
nums.sort(); res = []
def bt(start, path):
    res.append(path[:])
    for i in range(start, len(nums)):
        if i>start and nums[i]==nums[i-1]: continue
        path.append(nums[i]); bt(i+1, path); path.pop()
bt(0, []); return res

problems.name: Combination Sum II
problems.hint: Sort. Skip duplicates. Each element used at most once (i+1, not i).
problems.pseudocode:
candidates.sort(); res = []
def bt(start, path, total):
    if total==target: res.append(path[:]); return
    if total>target: return
    for i in range(start, len(candidates)):
        if i>start and candidates[i]==candidates[i-1]: continue
        path.append(candidates[i])
        bt(i+1, path, total+candidates[i])
        path.pop()
bt(0, [], 0); return res

problems.name: Letter Combinations of Phone Number
problems.hint: Map digits to letters. Backtrack: at each digit, try every letter it maps to.

problems.name: Word Search
problems.hint: Backtrack from each cell. Mark visited with '#' or use a visited set. Restore on backtrack.
problems.pseudocode:
def bt(i, j, k):
    if k==len(word): return True
    if i<0 or i>=m or j<0 or j>=n or board[i][j]!=word[k]: return False
    tmp = board[i][j]; board[i][j]='#'
    for di,dj in [(1,0),(-1,0),(0,1),(0,-1)]:
        if bt(i+di, j+dj, k+1): return True
    board[i][j]=tmp
    return False

problems.name: Palindrome Partitioning
problems.hint: Precompute isPalindrome table. Backtrack: at each position, try every palindrome-ending.

problems.name: N-Queens
problems.hint: Place queens row by row. Track columns, diagonals (r+c, r-c) under attack.
problems.pseudocode:
cols = set(); diag1 = set(); diag2 = set()
def bt(row, board):
    if row==n: res.append(board[:]); return
    for c in range(n):
        if c in cols or row+c in diag1 or row-c in diag2: continue
        cols.add(c); diag1.add(row+c); diag2.add(row-c)
        bt(row+1, board + ['.'*c+'Q'+'.'*(n-c-1)])
        cols.remove(c); diag1.remove(row+c); diag2.remove(row-c)

problems.hint: Build trie from words. DFS the board following trie paths. Mark word-end when reached.
problems.pseudocode:

problems.name: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II
problems.hint: Sum every positive daily difference (price[i] - price[i-1] if positive).

problems.name: Jump Game
problems.hint: Track maxReach. If current index > maxReach, return false.
problems.pseudocode:
maxReach = 0
for i in range(len(nums)):
    if i > maxReach: return False
    maxReach = max(maxReach, i + nums[i])
return True

problems.name: Jump Game II
problems.hint: BFS levels. Track current jump's farthest reach. When i hits end of level, jump count++.

problems.name: Gas Station
problems.hint: If total gas ≥ total cost, a solution exists. Greedily find the start: reset start when running sum < 0.
problems.pseudocode:
total = 0; tank = 0; start = 0
for i in range(n):
    total += gas[i] - cost[i]
    tank += gas[i] - cost[i]
    if tank < 0:
        start = i+1; tank = 0
return start if total >= 0 else -1

problems.name: Merge Intervals
problems.hint: Sort by start. Merge when current overlaps last in result.

problems.name: Non-overlapping Intervals
problems.hint: Sort by end time. Greedily keep the interval that ends earliest. Count removed.
problems.pseudocode:
intervals.sort(key=lambda x: x[1])
end = -INF; removed = 0
for s, e in intervals:
    if s >= end: end = e
    else: removed += 1
return removed

problems.name: Meeting Rooms II
problems.hint: Sort by start. Min-heap of end times. Heap size = rooms in use. Max size = answer.

problems.name: Task Scheduler
problems.hint: Max frequency task dictates shape. Slots = (maxFreq-1)*(n+1) + count of tasks with maxFreq.
problems.pseudocode:
counts = list(Counter(tasks).values())
maxFreq = max(counts)
maxCount = counts.count(maxFreq)
return max(len(tasks), (maxFreq-1)*(n+1) + maxCount)

problems.name: Partition Labels
problems.hint: Precompute last occurrence of each char. Extend current partition's end as you scan.

problems.name: Two City Scheduling
problems.hint: Sort by (costA - costB). First half goes to A, second half to B.

problems.name: Boats to Save People
problems.hint: Sort. Try to pair lightest with heaviest. If they fit together, both go; else only heaviest.
problems.pseudocode:
people.sort()
l, r = 0, len(people)-1
boats = 0
while l <= r:
    if people[l] + people[r] <= limit: l += 1
    r -= 1; boats += 1
return boats

problems.name: Queue Reconstruction by Height
problems.hint: Sort by height desc, then k asc. Insert each person at position k.
problems.pseudocode:
people.sort(key=lambda x: (-x[0], x[1]))
res = []
for p in people:
    res.insert(p[1], p)
return res

problems.name: Last Stone Weight
problems.hint: Max-heap. Pop two largest, smash, push back if non-zero. Repeat until ≤1 stone.
problems.pseudocode:
import heapq
heap = [-s for s in stones]
heapq.heapify(heap)
while len(heap) > 1:
    y = -heapq.heappop(heap)
    x = -heapq.heappop(heap)
    if y > x: heapq.heappush(heap, -(y-x))
return -heap[0] if heap else 0

problems.name: Kth Largest Element in Array
problems.hint: Maintain min-heap of size k. Heap top = kth largest.
problems.pseudocode:
import heapq
heap = nums[:k]
heapq.heapify(heap)
for n in nums[k:]:
    if n > heap[0]:
        heapq.heappushpop(heap, n)
return heap[0]

problems.name: K Closest Points to Origin
problems.hint: Max-heap of size k by distance. Evict farthest when heap is full and closer point found.

problems.name: Top K Frequent Elements
problems.hint: Min-heap of size k by frequency. Or bucket sort.
problems.pseudocode:
count = Counter(nums)
return heapq.nlargest(k, count.keys(), key=count.get)

problems.name: Task Scheduler
problems.hint: Max-heap of task counts. Each cycle: pop, decrement, push back after cooldown.
problems.pseudocode:

# Each round: pop up to n+1 tasks, decrement, push back if >0
# Count rounds; if heap empty, done

problems.hint: HashMap: user → list of tweets (with timestamp). Get feed: merge K sorted lists via heap.
problems.pseudocode:

# postTweet(userId, tweetId): tweets[userId].append((time, tweetId))
# getNewsFeed(userId): min-heap merge of latest tweets from user + followees
#   pop 10 most recent

problems.name: Kth Smallest Element in Sorted Matrix
problems.hint: Min-heap of size min(k, n). Push first element of each row. Pop k times.
problems.pseudocode:
import heapq
n = len(matrix)
heap = [(matrix[i][0], i, 0) for i in range(min(k, n))]
heapq.heapify(heap)
for _ in range(k-1):
    v, r, c = heapq.heappop(heap)
    if c+1 < n: heapq.heappush(heap, (matrix[r][c+1], r, c+1))
return heap[0][0]

problems.name: Find Median from Data Stream
problems.hint: Two heaps: max-heap lo (smaller half) and min-heap hi (larger half). Balance sizes.
problems.pseudocode:

problems.name: Sliding Window Maximum
problems.hint: Monotonic decreasing deque of indices. Front always = index of current window's max.
